title: friesodielsia alpina
intro: Friesodielsia alpina, also known as the "Umbrella Tree," is a plant species native to the tropical regions of the Pacific Islands, specifically in Fiji. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Friesodielsia alpina include treating various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, as well as being used as an antihelmintic to treat parasitic worms. Herbal preparations of Friesodielsia alpina often involve making a decoction from the roots or leaves, which can be consumed as a tea or applied topically to the affected area.
